skill.name: cto-advisor
skill.description: Technical leadership skill for CTO planning, architecture governance, technology evaluation, engineering metrics, technical debt assessment, and team scaling decisions.
skill.summary: Guide CTO decisions with practical engineering leadership frameworks and local planning tools.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown fences, ordinary leadership terminology, metric templates, and deterministic local Python examples. No evidence found of prompt injection, network access, secret access, dynamic code execution, subprocess use, or file exfiltration. The only residual concern is that the community skill includes local executable helper scripts.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and documentation snippets as Ruby backtick execution. Context shows local examples and templates, not dynamic shell execution or command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context shows fenced Markdown blocks and documented commands. No subprocess, os.system, popen, or Ruby execution construct was found.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain generic product, architecture, metrics, ratio, and planning terms. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts are prose, tables, and dictionary keys for planning tools. They do not implement or recommend weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged system and network terms appear in architecture planning, OKR templates, team scaling risks, and incident response guidance. No network scanner, host discovery, port probing, or environment reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are business and operations guidance, not executable reconnaissance logic. Searches found no networking libraries or scan commands.

low_findings.title: Bundled local Python scripts require normal community package caution
low_findings.description: The skill includes executable Python helper scripts for technical debt analysis and team scaling. They are deterministic calculators using standard libraries, but users should review community scripts before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are present and runnable, but review found no network calls, secret access, file writes, shell execution, or obfuscation.

content.user_title: Guide CTO Decisions with Engineering Frameworks
content.value_statement: Engineering leaders need consistent ways to assess debt, scale teams, and choose technologies. This skill provides CTO-ready frameworks, templates, and local calculators for structured technical planning.
actual_capabilities: Assess technical debt across architecture, code quality, infrastructure, security, and performance dimensions.
Estimate engineering team growth needs, hiring priorities, budget impact, and team structure options.
Provide architecture decision record templates and example decision workflows.
Define engineering metrics, DORA targets, OKRs, reporting templates, and dashboard examples.
Compare technologies and vendors with weighted evaluation criteria and proof-of-concept templates.
limitations: It does not connect to repositories, observability tools, or HR systems automatically.
Calculator output depends on user-provided assumptions and should be reviewed by leadership.
It provides strategic guidance, not legal, financial, or compliance advice.
The included Python scripts are local examples and are not packaged production applications.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Technical Debt Reduction
use_cases.description: Score debt categories, identify the highest-impact remediation areas, and create a capacity plan for cleanup work.

use_cases.title: Plan Engineering Team Growth
use_cases.description: Estimate hiring needs, role mix, management layers, onboarding pressure, and budget for a growing engineering group.

use_cases.title: Standardize Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Create ADRs, compare options, document consequences, and maintain decision history for technical governance.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Technical Debt Area
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a focused debt review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the CTO advisor skill to assess technical debt for this system. Focus on architecture, code quality, infrastructure, security, and performance. Ask me for missing inputs before scoring.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Team Scaling Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for hiring and structure planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the team scaling framework to plan growth from our current engineering headcount to our target headcount. Include role gaps, quarterly hiring priorities, onboarding risks, and budget assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Technology Evaluation Matrix
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for comparing vendors or platforms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the technology evaluation framework to compare these options. Weight technical, business, and operational criteria, identify decision risks, and propose a proof-of-concept plan.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Engineering Operating System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for CTO-level operating cadence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the CTO advisor references to design an engineering operating system. Include DORA metrics, quality metrics, OKRs, ADR governance, reporting cadence, and escalation paths.

output_examples.input: Assess technical debt for a legacy commerce platform with low test coverage and manual deployments.
output_examples.output: A prioritized debt report with category scores, risk level, remediation actions, effort estimates, and leadership recommendations.

output_examples.input: Plan growth from 25 engineers to 75 engineers over four quarters.
output_examples.output: A quarterly hiring plan with role priorities, management structure, budget projection, onboarding risks, and scaling recommendations.

output_examples.input: Compare Kubernetes, ECS, and Nomad for a new container platform decision.
output_examples.output: A weighted evaluation matrix, ADR-ready decision rationale, proof-of-concept checklist, and documented tradeoffs.

best_practices: Provide current team size, role mix, timeline, business goals, and constraints before asking for planning output.
Treat scores as decision support and review assumptions with engineering, product, finance, and security stakeholders.
Convert recommendations into ADRs, OKRs, owners, milestones, and measurable follow-up actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the calculators as the only basis for hiring, budget, or architecture decisions.
Do not hide uncertain inputs. Mark assumptions clearly so leaders can challenge them.
Do not optimize a single metric without balancing quality, reliability, delivery, and team health.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with CTO-level planning for technical debt, team scaling, architecture decisions, engineering metrics, and technology evaluation.

faq.question: Does it run external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access or external service calls. The included Python scripts run locally with example inputs.

faq.question: Can it analyze my repository automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides frameworks and calculators. You need to supply system details, metrics, and context.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-CTO roles?
faq.answer: Yes. Engineering managers, staff engineers, platform leads, and product leaders can use the planning templates.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output quality?
faq.answer: Useful inputs include headcount, role mix, delivery metrics, incident history, debt indicators, roadmap goals, and budget constraints.

faq.question: Should I run the bundled scripts?
faq.answer: Review community scripts before running them. This audit found no malicious behavior, but normal local execution caution applies.
